business.headline: OpenAI Closes Record 22 Billion Funding Round at 52 Billion Valuation
business.excerpt: OpenAI has completed one of the largest funding rounds in tech history, raising 22 billion at an 52 billion valuation...
business.takeaway: OpenAI's record 22 billion funding round signals Silicon Valley's unprecedented bet on AI infrastructure and sets the stage for the company's long-awaited IPO.

pull_quote.text: Enterprise revenue now makes up more than 40% of revenue, and is on track to reach parity with consumer by the end of 2026.

business.headline: Oracle Cuts Up to 30,000 Jobs as AI Infrastructure Spending Accelerates
business.excerpt: Oracle eliminated up to 30,000 employees on March 31, roughly 18% of its workforce, to fund a 56 billion AI infrastructure buildout.
business.takeaway: Oracle is eliminating up to 30,000 employees to fund a 56 billion AI data center buildout, freeing -10 billion in cash flow.

pull_quote.text: If an organisation cuts labour costs in order to build a factory—or, in this case, an AI data centre—that company is making a bet on its ability to deliver future revenue and profits.

business.headline: Anthropic Revenue Surges to 9 Billion ARR as Claude Code Drives Growth
business.excerpt: Anthropic has reached 9 billion in annualized revenue, a near-doubling from  billion at year-end 2025.
business.takeaway: Anthropic's revenue has doubled in two months to 9 billion, driven by Claude Code's explosive enterprise adoption.

pull_quote.text: Claude is increasingly becoming critical to how businesses work.

business.headline: Q1 2026 Venture Funding Shatters Records at 00 Billion, AI Captures 80%
business.excerpt: Global investors poured 00 billion into 6,000 startups in Q1 2026, with AI capturing 80% of all capital.
business.takeaway: Q1 2026 marked an unprecedented moment for venture capital with 00 billion invested and AI capturing 80% of all funding.

pull_quote.text: High-capital moats in compute-intensive and model-heavy startups create significant barriers to entry.

business.headline: Disney Scraps  Billion Sora Investment After OpenAI Announces Shutdown
business.excerpt: Disney abandons its  billion investment in OpenAI as the company shuts down Sora, exposing AI video economics.
business.takeaway: Disney abandons its  billion Sora investment, exposing cracks in AI video generation's economics.

pull_quote.text: As we focus and compute demand grows, the Sora research team continues to focus on world simulation research to advance robotics.

llms-genai.headline: Google Launches Veo 3.1 Lite, Slashes AI Video Costs by 50 Percent
llms-genai.excerpt: Google enters a new phase of AI democratization with Veo 3.1 Lite, positioning itself as the dominant video generation player.
llms-genai.takeaway: Google's Veo 3.1 Lite democratizes AI video generation by cutting costs in half as OpenAI exits the market.

pull_quote.text: Our commitment to making video generation more available to developers doesn't stop here.

llms-genai.headline: OpenAI Shuts Down Sora After Burning  Million Per Day
llms-genai.excerpt: OpenAI shuts down Sora just months after launch, revealing the brutal economics of compute-intensive AI products.
llms-genai.takeaway: OpenAI's Sora shutdown marks a watershed moment, revealing the brutal economics of compute-intensive products when user adoption fails.

pull_quote.text: The app was burning through roughly  million every day—not because people loved it but because video generation is so costly to run.

llms-genai.headline: OpenAI Extends Responses API With Shell Tool and Agent Loop
llms-genai.excerpt: OpenAI is fundamentally transforming how developers build autonomous agents with new Responses API capabilities.
llms-genai.takeaway: OpenAI's Responses API now enables developers to build autonomous agents with shell tools and execution loops.

pull_quote.text: The shell tool enables a much wider range of use cases, like running Go or Java programs or starting a NodeJS server.

llms-genai.headline: Google Unveils Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite With 2.5x Faster Response Times
llms-genai.excerpt: Google launches Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite, prioritizing speed and affordability for high-volume AI workloads.
llms-genai.takeaway: Google's Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite delivers 2.5x speed boost at 1/8th the cost of Pro, establishing a new efficiency standard.

pull_quote.text: Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite reaches its first response token 2.5 times faster than Gemini 2.5 Flash and pushes out over 360 tokens per second.

llms-genai.headline: Huawei Rolls Out 950PR AI Chip as ByteDance and Alibaba Order
llms-genai.excerpt: Huawei rolls out its Ascend 950PR AI chip with backing from China's tech heavyweights.
llms-genai.takeaway: Huawei's 950PR gains traction with major Chinese tech firms as a domestic alternative to Nvidia, targeting 750K shipments in 2026.

pull_quote.text: The company built a translation layer that lets engineers keep using the tools they know without rewriting their work.

research.headline: Allen Institute Theorizer Writes Testable Scientific Theories at 0.90 Precision
research.excerpt: The Allen Institute for AI unveiled Theorizer, a multi-LLM framework that transforms scientific theory building.
research.takeaway: Theorizer automates theory generation by reading thousands of papers and producing structured, testable theories with 90% accuracy.

pull_quote.text: A way for scientists to get oriented in a new domain in minutes rather than months.

research.headline: Physics-Informed ML Breakthrough Advances Climate Modeling Accuracy
research.excerpt: Physics-informed neural networks are revolutionizing climate modeling by embedding fundamental physical laws into AI systems.
research.takeaway: Physics-informed neural networks achieve 40% error reductions in precipitation forecasting while dramatically reducing computational costs.

pull_quote.text: Physics-informed neural networks preserve the rigor of traditional climate science while capturing nonlinear dynamics that pure numerical models miss.

research.headline: AI Diagnostic Systems Now Match Veteran Radiologists in Imaging Analysis
research.excerpt: AI systems have matched the diagnostic accuracy of experienced radiologists across MRI, CT, and X-ray imaging in 2026.
research.takeaway: AI systems have crossed a critical threshold, matching radiologist performance across MRI, CT, and X-ray imaging.

pull_quote.text: Prima represents a new era of AI-assisted radiology, delivering stronger diagnostic performance across more than 50 different radiologic diagnoses.

research.headline: Unit 42 Discovers High-Severity Vulnerability in Chrome Gemini Live Panel
research.excerpt: Security researchers uncovered a critical flaw allowing malicious extensions to hijack Chrome's Gemini AI panel.
research.takeaway: Researchers found a critical flaw allowing malicious extensions to hijack Chrome's Gemini AI panel for camera, mic, and file access.

pull_quote.text: An extension influencing a website is expected. However, an extension influencing a component that is baked into the browser is a serious security risk.

policy.headline: Federal Judge Rules Trump Anthropic Ban Violates Free Speech
policy.excerpt: A federal judge blocks the Trump administration's ban on Anthropic AI in government, citing First Amendment violations.
policy.takeaway: A federal judge halted the ban on Anthropic AI in government, ruling it constitutes illegal First Amendment retaliation.

pull_quote.text: The Orwellian notion that an American company may be branded a potential adversary and saboteur of the U.S. for expressing disagreement with the government.

policy.headline: White House Issues Seven-Pillar National AI Policy Framework
policy.excerpt: The Trump administration unveils an AI policy framework establishing seven pillars while pushing federal preemption of state laws.
policy.takeaway: The White House proposes aggressive federal preemption of state AI laws with seven pillars focused on child protection and innovation.

pull_quote.text: The Trump administration understands that light-touch regulation enabled the internet revolution.

policy.headline: EU Council Agrees to Delay High-Risk AI Rules by 16 Months
policy.excerpt: The EU Council approved a 16-month extension for high-risk AI compliance, pushing deadlines to late 2027-2028.
policy.takeaway: The EU Council approved a 16-month extension for high-risk AI compliance after standardization bodies missed critical deadlines.

pull_quote.text: The delays to publishing standards and frameworks required for implementing parts of the AI Act.
